PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-05-2016 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Nov-17-2016 Continued To Dec-05-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mona Lisa Wallace for and with (petitioner) Sylvia Sommer.

The Court asked for additional argument on the Spousal Property Petition. Ms. Williams reported that Ms. Sommer survived her husband, that it was an untimely death, that there is no will, that the siblings have signed declarations stating that they understood the house to be community property, that they are asking the Court to order that the property be transferred, that it will avoid probate, that there is no debt to the estate and that the family is in agreement. The Court asked for additional legal authority. Ms. Wallace stated that she can do additional research, that they have not seen a precedent, that the Court can grant it on its equitable powers and that appraisal fees would be unnecessary as Ms. Sommer plans to reside in the property. The Court stated that it will give counsel one week to provide additional argument and that the Petition for Letters of Administration and the Petition for Spousal Property will be under submission as of October 3, 2016. The Court requested that counsel provide a proposed order on the Petition for Letters of Administration.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not Reported)
